title: (1D) Ordered Tokens Enable Efficient Test-Time Search
gist: Apple/EPFL show that 1D coarse-to-fine token structures (vs classical 2D grid tokens) make autoregressive image generators far more amenable to test-time search, even enabling training-free text-to-image generation via pure search guided by a verifier.

insights.claim: The internal structure of tokens, not just the autoregressive model, determines how effectively test-time search can steer generative output, because coarse-to-fine intermediate states carry semantic meaning a verifier can reliably evaluate mid-generation.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Signals that inference-time compute gains in generative AI depend on representation design, not only bigger models or more search compute.
insights.quote: the intermediate states in coarse-to-fine sequences carry semantic meaning that verifiers can reliably evaluate, enabling effective steering during generation

insights.claim: Autoregressive image models trained on 1D coarse-to-fine ordered tokens exhibit better test-time scaling behavior than grid-based (2D) counterparts under controlled experiments.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Favors 1D tokenizer research (e.g., TiTok-style) as the path to squeezing more quality from fixed inference budgets.
insights.quote: AR models trained on coarse-to-fine ordered tokens exhibit improved test-time scaling behavior compared to grid-based counterparts

insights.claim: With ordered token structure, pure test-time search over token sequences can perform text-to-image generation without training any autoregressive model at all, when guided by an image-text verifier.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Points to a paradigm where verifiers plus search substitute for expensive generative training, lowering the compute moat for some generation tasks.
insights.quote: pure test-time search over token sequences (i.e., without training an AR model) can perform training-free text-to-image generation when guided by an image-text verifier

insights.claim: Classical search algorithms (best-of-N, beam search, lookahead search) interact differently with different token structures, and the choice of verifier and AR prior materially affects inference-time scalability.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Gives practitioners a decision framework for pairing search strategy with representation to maximize inference-time returns.
insights.quote: we systematically study how classical search algorithms (best-of-N, beam search, lookahead search) interact with different token structures
